items.q: What does modulation mean in music?
items.correct: Changing from one key to another within a piece.
items.why: Students conflate modulation with any musical change. Specifically, modulation is a KEY CHANGE, usually established by a perfect cadence in the new key - a structural device.

items.q: Which chords form a plagal cadence?
items.why: Students confuse plagal (IV-I) with perfect (V-I) because both end on I. The 'amen' at the end of hymns is the classic plagal cadence - subdominant to tonic, with a softer ecclesiastical feel.

items.q: How many crotchets are in a semibreve?
items.why: Students confuse the relative durations. A semibreve (whole note) = 2 minims = 4 crotchets = 8 quavers. Each level halves in length.
Correct answer: Alto sax. “Trumpet” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The answer examiners want is: 1945. “1935” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Charlie Parker lived 1920 to 1955. What was his age at death (in years)?
items.why: The real figure is 34. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
